title: Yellappa S/O. Revaneppa Dollin, vs Hanumappa S/O. Revaneppa Dollin, on 19 October, 2012
body: Yellappa S/O. Revaneppa Dollin, vs Hanumappa S/O. Revaneppa Dollin, on 19 October, 2012 Author: S.N.Satyanarayana Bench: S. N. Satyanarayana 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA CIRCUIT BENCH AT DHARWAD DATED THIS THE 19TH DAY OF OCTOBER 2012 BEFORE THE HON'BLE MR.JUSTICE S. N. SATYANARAYANA MSA NO.316/2011 (LAC) BETWEEN: Yellappa, S/o Revaneppa Dollin, age: 40 years. Occ: Agriculture. r/o Koppal, Tq. & Dist: Koppal. ... Appellant (By Sri. P. G. Mogli, Adv.) AND: 1. Hanumappa, S/o Revaneppa Dollin, Age: 49 years. r/o Near Sirasappayyanmath. At: Tq & Dist: Koppal. 2. Shrishailappa, S/o Revaneppa Dollin, Age: 35 years. R/o - do - 3. Smt Dyamawwa, W/o Kariyappa, Age: 33 years. R/o Malakapur, Tq: Sindhanoor, Dist: Raichur. 2 4. Smt. Nagamma, W/o Gavisiddappa Bellary. Age: 31 years. R/o Kataraki, Tq. & Dist: Koppal. .. Respondents. (By Sri.Chandrashekar P. Patil, Adv. for R.1 & R.2, R.3-left, R.4 served and unrepresented) This MSA is filed under Order XLIII Rule 1(u) of CPC , praying to set-aside the judgment and decree passed by the learned Senior Civil Judge, Koppal, in R.A.No.60/2010 dated 17.2.2011 and restore the judgment and decree passed in O. S.10 /2005 dated 27.10.2010 passed by the Civil Judge & JMFC, Koppal, and etc. This MSA coming on for Orders, this day, the Court delivered the following: JUDGMENT This appeal is filed by the respondent in R.A.60/2010 on the file of the Civil Judge (Sr.Dn), Koppal. The said Regular Appeal arises out of the original suit in O. S.10 /2006 dated 27.10.10 on the file of the Civil Judge (Jr.Dn), Koppal. 2. The parties in O. S.10 /2006 and in O. S.121 /07 are one and the same. The suit in O. S.10 /2006 is for the relief of partition and separate possession, whereas the suit in O. S.121 /07 is for declaration. 3 3. It is stated that the aforesaid two suits came to be dismissed on 27.10.2010 by separate judgments, against which two appeals were filed in RA.59/2010 and R.A.60/2010. R.A.59/10 is filed by the plaintiff in OS.121/07 challenging the dismissal of the said suit, whereas R.A.60/2010 is filed by the defendants in O. S.10 /2007. It is seen that R.A.59/2010 is dismissed thereby confirming the judgment and decree passed in O. S.121 /07. In so far as R.A.60/10 is concerned which is filed by the very same persons who are defendants in O. S.10 /06 the matter was remanded back to the Tribunal for inclusion of the joint family properties. 4. In so far as the judgment passed in R.A.60/10 it is challenged by both the parties. The appeal in MSA.312/2011 filed by the appellant in both the appeals is dismissed and the order of remand is confirmed. 5. The respondent in R.A.60/2010 challenging the very same order impugned in MSA.312/2011 has come up in this appeal MSA.316/2011. On going through the judgment passed in MSA.312/2011 it is seen that since the 4 very same judgment in R.A.60/2010 which is the subject matter of this appeal is already disposed of confirming the judgment of remand, the question of going through the judgment impugned once again and deciding the correctness or otherwise of the judgment passed in R.A.60/2010 vis-à-vis the judgment passed in MSA.312/11 does not arise, in as much as, it would result in sitting in judgment over the decision of very same Court. 6. In that view of the matter, following the judgment passed in MSA.312/11 this appeal also is dismissed. The judgment and decree passed by the Trial Court in R.A.60/2007 is confirmed in remanding the matter back to the Civil Judge (Jr.Dn), Koppal, to re-hear the suit for partition after inclusion of all the joint family properties which is under contest between the parties therein. Sd/- JUDGE Sub/
